signals.idea: Clean up video editor timeline engineering: split useTimelineEditing (~800 lines) into focused modules, remove inline styles from external drop indicators, clarify library callback coupling, remove unnecessary stopPropagation in ClipAction, replace shared mutable ActionDragState ref with cleaner coordination, fix EditorPaneTab generation pane height hack properly in getEffectivePaneHeights

unresolved_flags.concern: Removing `ClipAction`'s `onPointerDown` `stopPropagation` is not supported by the current event flow and risks reintroducing competing timeline-library drags.
Step 7 is under-scoped and internally inconsistent: the proposed coordinator still exposes the raw ref, so it would not actually eliminate direct `ActionDragState` mutation in this repository.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 3.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.

items: useTimelineEditing.ts reduced from ~947 lines to ~250 lines, with 4–5 focused sub-hooks
Zero inline styles on the drop indicator root element (moved to CSS class); dynamic position styles remain inline
Library callback return-value contracts documented with JSDoc on each callback
ActionDragState mutations go through a coordinator API — no direct ref.current[id] = ... outside the coordinator
ClipAction has exactly 2 stopPropagation calls (onClick, onDoubleClick) with explaining comments
EditorPaneTab has no local height subtraction hack — effectiveEditorPaneHeight from PanesContext is used directly
getEffectivePaneHeights handles generations-pane visibility, with updated tests
npm run build passes after every step
Manual smoke test: create/move/resize/drag-drop/delete/split clips all work

unresolved_flags.concern: Removing `ClipAction`'s `onPointerDown` `stopPropagation` is not supported by the current event flow and risks reintroducing competing timeline-library drags.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `ClipAction` currently blocks bubbled `pointerdown` at `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:45`. In `useClipDrag`, the wrapper listener runs in capture phase (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:425-426`), but pointer capture is only taken after the move threshold inside `handlePointerMove` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:318-323`). That means a bubbled `pointerdown` can still reach the timeline library before this hook has actually claimed the drag, so the plan's rationale that pointer capture already handles it is weaker than the repository code supports.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 7 is under-scoped and internally inconsistent: the proposed coordinator still exposes the raw ref, so it would not actually eliminate direct `ActionDragState` mutation in this repository.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan's proposed `ActionDragCoordinator` includes `ref: MutableRefObject<Record<string, ActionDragState>>`, but the success criteria say there should be no direct `ref.current[id] = ...` outside the coordinator. In the repo, that raw ref is part of the public plumbing already: `useTimelineData` exports `actionDragStateRef` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts:84`), `useTimelineState` exposes and forwards it (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:25`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:112`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts:149`), and `TimelineEditor` passes it into `useClipDrag` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:26`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:62`). Updating only `useLibraryCallbacks.ts` and `useClipDrag.ts` will not complete the migration.
